verdicts.note: The MCP page clearly documents three Ramp MCP servers: Ramp MCP (account-specific), Developer MCP (docs), and Ramp Data MCP (public benchmarks).
The URL points to Ramp's documentation page for their MCP servers, which is appropriate for documenting existing MCP availability.

The authorization page confirms OAuth 2.0 with Authorization Code, Refresh Token, and Client Credentials grants; token endpoint at /developer/v1/token; Basic auth encoding of client_id:client_secret; Bearer token usage.

The getting-started guide explicitly states 'This guide assumes you are a Ramp customer with admin access to your dashboard' and shows admin-level UI navigation required (Company → Developer). Sandbox requires a request. This confirms 'admin-approval' requirement.
The authorization and getting-started pages document REST endpoints (POST /developer/v1/token, GET /developer/v1/transactions, etc.).

The one-liner references 'spend/finance data' and the original record mentions transactions, cards, reimbursements, bills, users. The evidence shows transactions endpoint and references to scopes like 'transactions:read' which supports a broad API, though not all endpoints are enumerated in this evidence.

The getting-started page states 'This guide assumes you are a Ramp customer' and 'you'll need admin access.' Sandbox access must be requested. This supports 'buildable-with-caveats' since a developer needs a Ramp business account and admin approval.
The stated blocker matches the evidence: requires Ramp business account, admin access to create OAuth app, sandbox write operations disabled, and sandbox access itself requires a request.

The one-liner states 'broad OAuth2-secured REST Developer API for spend/finance data, plus separate MCP servers for documentation and account data access' which is confirmed by the evidence showing OAuth2, REST API, transaction/expense/bill scopes, and three MCP servers documented.

revised.name: Ramp
revised.one_liner: Ramp offers a broad OAuth2-secured REST Developer API for spend/finance data, plus separate MCP servers for documentation and account data access.

auth_methods.notes: OAuth 2.0 with Authorization Code, Refresh Token, and Client Credentials grants. Token endpoint at https://api.ramp.com/developer/v1/token, authenticated via HTTP Basic (base64 client_id:client_secret), returns Bearer access token.

revised.self_serve_notes: Requires a Ramp business account and app registration (client ID/secret) to obtain OAuth credentials; production API access may require admin permissions or a support/provisioning request. Sandbox available for testing without full production approval.

api_surface.notes: Ramp Developer API (v1) covers business financial data: transactions, cards, reimbursements, bills, users, etc. OpenAPI spec published at /openapi/developer-api.json.

revised.main_blocker: Production API access requires a Ramp business account, OAuth app registration, and potentially admin approval/support ticket for provisioning; write operations are disabled in sandbox.

evidence.supports: OAuth2 flow details: client credentials, authorization code, refresh token grants; Basic auth for token endpoint; Bearer token usage.
Existence of Ramp MCP server(s) - both account-specific ramp-mcp and unauthenticated Developer MCP for docs.
Setup guide referenced for obtaining OAuth credentials and getting started with API.
Developer API access tied to business account plans, not necessarily free self-serve tier.

flags: MCP server confirmed to exist (two variants: ramp-mcp and Developer MCP)
Self-serve status ambiguous - requires business account and possibly admin approval for production scopes
Sandbox environment available but write operations disabled, limiting testing depth
